description: Architecture decision with named component, approach, and stated purpose
source: We'll use Redis-backed session storage to enable horizontal scaling across API replicas. Sticky sessions on a single node would cap us at one instance and can't meet the 20k concurrent user SLA.
rationale: Subject is a component (Redis-backed session storage). Predicate names the approach AND the purpose (horizontal scaling). A fork exists: sticky sessions on a single node was the named alternative. Gate 1 passes: '20k concurrent user SLA' is a named business SLA. Gate 2 passes: two teams might reasonably choose sticky-session vs. Redis.
